Title: Generate appointment reminder for a scheduled visit within reminder window
Preconditions: Logged in as Scheduler; patient has a future appointment scheduled and an active opt-in for reminders; reminder lead time configured to 24h
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient appointment record | 2. Confirm appointment time is within the 24h reminder window | 3. Trigger the reminder scheduler job | 4. Inspect the notification queue for the patient
Expected_Result: A single reminder notification is created in the queue for the patient with correct appointment date/time, channel SMS, and status Pending

Title: Suppress reminder when appointment is cancelled before send
Preconditions: Reminder is queued in Pending state for a future appointment; logged in as Scheduler
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm a Pending reminder exists for the appointment | 2. Cancel the appointment | 3. Re-run the reminder scheduler | 4. Inspect the reminder state
Expected_Result: Reminder transitions from Pending to Cancelled/Suppressed and is not delivered to the patient

Title: Reminder lead-time boundary at exactly the configured window edge
Preconditions: Reminder lead time configured to exactly 24h; appointment exists; logged in as Scheduler
Test_Steps: 1. Set appointment to exactly 24h00m00s from current time | 2. Run scheduler | 3. Set a second appointment to 24h00m01s | 4. Run scheduler | 5. Compare which appointments queued a reminder
Expected_Result: Appointment at exactly 24h is included in the reminder batch; appointment just outside the window is not, per inclusive boundary rule

Title: No duplicate reminder created when scheduler runs twice
Preconditions: An appointment is eligible for a reminder; reminder scheduler idempotency enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Run reminder scheduler once | 2. Confirm one Pending reminder exists | 3. Run scheduler again without state change | 4. Inspect queue count for the appointment
Expected_Result: Exactly one reminder exists; the second run does not create a duplicate notification

Title: Critical lab result triggers high-priority clinician alert
Preconditions: Logged in as Lab Tech; ordering provider on record; critical-result thresholds configured for Potassium
Test_Steps: 1. Enter a lab result above the critical-high threshold | 2. Save the result | 3. Open the ordering provider notification inbox | 4. Verify alert content
Test_Data: Patient=Synthetic A; Test=Potassium; Value=7.2 mmol/L; CriticalHigh=>6.0
Expected_Result: A critical-result alert is generated for the ordering provider with priority Critical, the abnormal value, and reference range, in real time

Title: Critical alert escalates when not acknowledged within SLA
Preconditions: Critical-result alert delivered to primary provider; escalation SLA configured to 15 minutes; backup provider defined
Test_Steps: 1. Deliver a critical alert to the primary provider | 2. Do not acknowledge | 3. Advance time beyond the 15-minute SLA | 4. Run escalation job | 5. Inspect alert routing
Expected_Result: Unacknowledged alert escalates from primary provider to the backup/on-call provider and is logged with an escalation timestamp

Title: Acknowledgement state transition from New to Acknowledged
Preconditions: A critical alert exists in New state in the provider inbox; logged in as that provider
Test_Steps: 1. Open the New critical alert | 2. Click Acknowledge | 3. Confirm acknowledgement | 4. Inspect alert state and audit log
Expected_Result: Alert transitions New -> Acknowledged, escalation timer is stopped, and an audit entry records the acknowledging user and timestamp

Title: Invalid state transition from Acknowledged back to New is rejected
Preconditions: An alert is already in Acknowledged state; logged in as provider
Test_Steps: 1. Open the Acknowledged alert | 2. Attempt to set the alert back to New via UI/API | 3. Inspect the response and the alert state
Expected_Result: The system rejects the illegal transition; alert remains Acknowledged and an error is returned; no audit corruption occurs

Title: Drug-allergy alert fires when ordering a contraindicated medication
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient has a documented penicillin allergy; CDS allergy checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Open the patient order entry | 2. Order Amoxicillin | 3. Observe the interaction/allergy check | 4. Read the alert details
Expected_Result: A drug-allergy alert is presented before order confirmation, naming the allergen, severity, and the conflicting medication

Title: Drug-drug interaction alert with override reason capture
Preconditions: Logged in as Provider; patient already on Warfarin; interaction checking enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order Aspirin for the patient | 2. Observe interaction alert | 3. Choose to override | 4. Attempt to proceed without an override reason | 5. Enter a valid override reason and proceed
Test_Data: Patient on Warfarin; New order=Aspirin; Severity=Major
Expected_Result: Interaction alert appears; override is blocked until a reason is entered; with a valid reason the order proceeds and the override reason is stored and auditable

Title: Duplicate identical allergy alert is suppressed within dedup window
Preconditions: Patient with penicillin allergy; same contraindicated drug ordered twice in one session; dedup window configured
Test_Steps: 1. Order Amoxicillin and trigger the allergy alert | 2. Dismiss/acknowledge the alert | 3. Re-order the same Amoxicillin in the same session within the dedup window | 4. Observe alerting behavior
Expected_Result: The second identical alert is deduplicated/suppressed or shown as already-acknowledged rather than presented as a brand-new interruptive alert

Title: Distinct alerts for different drugs are not collapsed by dedup logic
Preconditions: Patient with multiple allergies; dedup enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Order Drug A and trigger an allergy alert | 2. Order Drug B with a different allergen conflict | 3. Verify both alerts are surfaced
Expected_Result: Both distinct allergy alerts are presented; dedup does not incorrectly suppress the second, clinically different alert

Title: Display in-app system maintenance banner to all logged-in users
Preconditions: Logged in as System Administrator; a system message broadcast feature is available
Test_Steps: 1. Create a system message with start/end window | 2. Activate the broadcast | 3. Log in as a clinical user | 4. Verify the banner is shown
Expected_Result: All active users see the system message banner during the active window; the banner is dismissible and does not block clinical workflow

Title: OpenMRS alert is delivered to a specific user role inbox
Preconditions: Logged in as Administrator on OpenMRS RefApp; target user account exists
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to the Manage Alerts admin screen | 2. Create an alert addressed to a specific user | 3. Save the alert | 4. Log in as the target user | 5. Open the alert/notification area
Expected_Result: The targeted user sees the alert in their notification area; non-targeted users do not see it

Title: Mark OpenMRS alert as read removes it from active alert list
Preconditions: Logged in as a user who has at least one unread alert in OpenMRS
Test_Steps: 1. Open the alert list | 2. Confirm the alert shows as unread | 3. Mark the alert as read | 4. Refresh the alert list
Expected_Result: The alert is marked read for that user and no longer appears in the active/unread alert list; state persists across sessions

Title: Email delivery of appointment reminder succeeds with valid address
Preconditions: Patient opted in to email reminders with a valid email; SMTP/email gateway configured
Test_Steps: 1. Queue an email reminder for the patient | 2. Trigger the delivery worker | 3. Inspect delivery status and gateway response | 4. Verify message content
Expected_Result: Email reminder is sent, delivery status is Delivered/Sent, and the message contains correct appointment details

Title: SMS delivery to invalid phone number is handled with failure status
Preconditions: Patient opted in to SMS reminders but has a malformed phone number; SMS gateway configured
Test_Steps: 1. Queue an SMS reminder | 2. Trigger delivery | 3. Inspect gateway response and reminder status | 4. Verify error handling
Expected_Result: Delivery fails gracefully with status Failed and a captured gateway error; the failure is logged and does not crash the worker

Title: Failed delivery is retried per backoff policy up to max attempts
Preconditions: Delivery worker configured with retries=3 and exponential backoff; gateway returns transient failure
Test_Steps: 1. Queue a notification | 2. Force a transient gateway error | 3. Allow the worker to retry | 4. Count attempts and inspect timing | 5. Observe terminal state after max attempts
Expected_Result: Notification is retried up to exactly 3 times with increasing delays; after the final failed attempt it moves to a terminal Failed/Dead-letter state

Title: Channel fallback from SMS to email on persistent SMS failure
Preconditions: Patient opted in to SMS with email as fallback; SMS gateway down
Test_Steps: 1. Queue reminder with channel preference SMS then Email | 2. Force SMS to fail all retries | 3. Observe fallback routing | 4. Verify final delivery channel
Expected_Result: After SMS exhausts retries, the system falls back to Email and the reminder is delivered via Email with the fallback recorded

Title: Patient opts out of SMS reminders and no SMS is sent
Preconditions: Patient currently opted in to SMS reminders; logged in as patient or registration staff updating consent
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient communication preferences | 2. Set SMS reminders to Opt-Out | 3. Save preferences | 4. Queue an appointment reminder | 5. Inspect delivery channels
Expected_Result: No SMS is sent after opt-out; only the remaining opted-in channels are used; the consent change is timestamped and auditable

Title: Opt-out does not suppress mandatory critical safety alerts to clinicians
Preconditions: Patient has opted out of marketing/reminder communications; a critical clinical alert is generated for clinicians
Test_Steps: 1. Confirm patient opt-out status for reminders | 2. Generate a critical-result alert tied to that patient | 3. Verify clinician alerting
Test_Data: Patient opt-out=reminders; Alert=Critical lab to provider
Expected_Result: Patient-facing reminder opt-out does NOT suppress clinician-directed critical safety alerts; the provider still receives the critical alert

Title: Opt-out preference persists across sessions and is not overwritten by defaults
Preconditions: Patient previously set SMS Opt-Out; database access available to verify persisted preference
Test_Steps: 1. Verify SMS preference is Opt-Out in the preference store | 2. Log out and back in | 3. Re-open preferences | 4. Query the persisted preference record
Expected_Result: The persisted opt-out value is unchanged after re-login; default seeding logic does not overwrite an explicit patient choice

Title: Create FHIR Communication resource for a delivered notification
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint available via the Resource Adapter Layer; OAuth token with write scope
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Communication resource representing a sent reminder | 2. Capture the resource id | 3. GET the resource | 4. Validate required fields
Expected_Result: The Communication resource is created with HTTP 201, a server-assigned id, status completed, and valid recipient/payload references per FHIR R4

Title: Validate FHIR CommunicationRequest for a queued reminder
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint available; reminder queued as a CommunicationRequest
Test_Steps: 1. GET the CommunicationRequest for the queued reminder | 2. Validate status and intent | 3. Validate occurrence timing and recipient
Expected_Result: CommunicationRequest validates against FHIR R4: status active, intent present, occurrence timing matches the appointment, recipient references the patient

Title: Reject FHIR Communication with missing required status element
Preconditions: FHIR endpoint with validation enabled
Test_Steps: 1. POST a Communication resource omitting the required status element | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: Server returns HTTP 400/422 with an OperationOutcome indicating the missing required status; the invalid resource is not persisted

Title: Generate HL7 v2 result message that triggers critical-result notification
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured to receive ORU^R01 result messages; critical thresholds mapped
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU^R01 message with an abnormal flag for a critical analyte | 2. Process the message | 3. Verify a critical-result alert is generated
Expected_Result: The ORU^R01 with abnormal flag HH is parsed and triggers a critical-result notification to the ordering provider

Title: Malformed HL7 message segment is rejected and ACK reflects error
Preconditions: HL7 v2 interface configured
Test_Steps: 1. Send an ORU message with a malformed/missing required OBX segment | 2. Process the message | 3. Inspect the ACK
Expected_Result: The interface returns an application error ACK (AE/AR); no spurious alert is generated; the error is logged for review

Title: Retrieve unacknowledged alerts via API for a provider
Preconditions: REST API available; valid auth token for a provider with pending alerts
Test_Steps: 1. Call GET /notifications?status=unacknowledged with the provider token | 2. Inspect the response payload | 3. Verify pagination and counts
Expected_Result: API returns 200 with the provider's unacknowledged alerts, correct total count, and pagination metadata; no other provider's alerts are leaked

Title: Acknowledge alert via API updates state and is idempotent
Preconditions: REST API available; an unacknowledged alert id known; valid provider token
Test_Steps: 1. POST acknowledge for the alert id | 2. Verify state becomes Acknowledged | 3. POST acknowledge again for the same id | 4. Inspect response
Expected_Result: First acknowledge returns 200 and sets Acknowledged; second call is idempotent (200/409 without corrupting state); audit records a single acknowledgement

Title: Provider cannot acknowledge another provider's alert (authorization)
Preconditions: REST API available; alert belongs to Provider A; valid token for Provider B
Test_Steps: 1. As Provider B, call acknowledge on Provider A's alert id | 2. Inspect the response and the alert state
Test_Data: alert_id owned by Provider A; token=Provider B
Expected_Result: API returns 403 Forbidden (or 404); the alert remains owned by and unacknowledged for Provider A; the attempt is logged

Title: Unauthenticated request to notifications endpoint is rejected
Preconditions: REST API available; no auth token supplied
Test_Steps: 1. Call GET /notifications without an Authorization header | 2. Inspect the response
Expected_Result: API returns 401 Unauthorized; no notification data including PHI is returned in the body

Title: Audit log captures who acknowledged a critical alert and when
Preconditions: Audit logging enabled; a critical alert has been acknowledged by a provider
Test_Steps: 1. Acknowledge a critical alert as a provider | 2. Query the audit log/table | 3. Verify the recorded fields
Expected_Result: Audit log contains an immutable entry with user id, alert id, action Acknowledge, and UTC timestamp; entries cannot be edited via the UI

Title: SMS reminder content does not expose sensitive clinical details
Preconditions: Patient opted in to SMS reminders; message templates configured
Test_Steps: 1. Queue an SMS appointment reminder | 2. Inspect the rendered SMS body
Expected_Result: SMS body contains only minimum-necessary info (e.g., generic appointment time/location) and does not disclose diagnosis, test names, or other PHI beyond what is necessary

Title: Critical alert dialog is keyboard accessible and screen-reader announced
Preconditions: Critical alert UI available; keyboard-only and screen reader (e.g., NVDA) configured
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger a critical alert dialog | 2. Navigate the dialog using only the keyboard | 3. Confirm focus is trapped within the dialog | 4. Verify the screen reader announces the alert role and content
Expected_Result: Dialog is fully operable by keyboard, focus is trapped and returned on close, and the alert is announced with an appropriate ARIA alert/role per WCAG 2.1 AA

Title: Alert severity is not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Notification list UI showing alerts of varying severity
Test_Steps: 1. Open the notification list with critical, high, and low alerts | 2. Inspect how severity is indicated
Expected_Result: Severity is conveyed via text/label or icon in addition to color, satisfying WCAG use-of-color requirements for color-blind users

Title: Pairwise coverage of channel x opt-in x patient-contact validity
Preconditions: Patients configured with combinations of channel, opt-in status, and contact-info validity
Test_Steps: 1. For each pairwise combination, queue a reminder | 2. Trigger delivery | 3. Record delivery outcome | 4. Compare against expected
Expected_Result: Each pairwise combination yields the expected outcome: delivered only when opted-in and contact valid; suppressed when opted-out; failed/handled when contact invalid

Title: Decision table for alert routing by severity and acknowledgement SLA
Preconditions: Alert engine configured with severity-based routing and SLA rules
Test_Steps: 1. Generate alerts across severities | 2. Vary acknowledgement within/after SLA | 3. Observe routing, escalation, and dedup per rule | 4. Compare to the decision table
Expected_Result: Each rule combination produces the documented outcome: critical+after-SLA escalates, low+acknowledged closes, duplicates dedup; no rule yields an undefined result

Title: Boundary on max notification message length for SMS segment
Preconditions: SMS template configurable; gateway enforces segment limits
Test_Steps: 1. Render an SMS reminder at exactly 160 characters | 2. Render one at 161 characters | 3. Inspect segmentation and delivery
Expected_Result: 160-char message sends as a single segment; 161 chars either splits into multi-part per gateway rules or is rejected per configured policy without truncating critical info

Title: Time zone correctness of reminder send time across DST boundary
Preconditions: Patient and facility in a time zone observing DST; appointment scheduled near a DST change
Test_Steps: 1. Schedule an appointment local time near the DST transition | 2. Queue the reminder | 3. Compute the scheduled send time | 4. Verify against expected local time
Test_Data: TZ=America/New_York; Appt near DST change; Lead=24h
Expected_Result: Reminder is scheduled and displayed in the correct local time accounting for the DST shift; no off-by-one-hour error in send or displayed appointment time

Title: Exploratory session on high-volume critical alerts and inbox behavior
Preconditions: Test environment seeded with a large volume of alerts for one provider
Test_Steps: 1. Generate a burst of 200+ alerts for a single provider | 2. Explore inbox loading, sorting, filtering, and acknowledgement | 3. Probe for performance lag, ordering errors, lost acknowledgements | 4. Note any defects
Expected_Result: Inbox remains responsive; critical alerts are prioritized/visible; acknowledgements are not lost under load; any anomalies are documented with reproduction steps

Title: Acknowledgement requires authenticated active session (no auto-ack)
Preconditions: A critical alert is in New state; session may be expired
Test_Steps: 1. Let the provider session expire | 2. Attempt to acknowledge the alert | 3. Inspect behavior
Expected_Result: Acknowledgement is rejected and the user is prompted to re-authenticate; the alert remains New so it is not silently lost

Title: Dead-letter notification is visible for manual review and resend
Preconditions: A notification has reached terminal Failed/Dead-letter state; logged in as Notification Admin
Test_Steps: 1. Open the dead-letter/failed notifications view | 2. Locate the failed notification | 3. Inspect failure reason | 4. Trigger a manual resend
Expected_Result: Failed notification is listed with its failure reason; admin can manually resend, and a successful resend updates the status with an audit trail

Title: Double opt-in confirmation required before activating SMS reminders
Preconditions: Patient requests SMS reminders; double opt-in policy enabled
Test_Steps: 1. Patient requests SMS reminders | 2. System sends a confirmation request | 3a. Patient confirms -> verify activation | 3b. Patient does not confirm -> verify not activated
Expected_Result: SMS reminders are activated only after explicit confirmation; without confirmation the channel stays inactive and no marketing reminders are sent

Title: Concurrent acknowledgement of the same shared alert by two users
Preconditions: An alert is addressed to a role/queue shared by two users; both logged in
Test_Steps: 1. User A opens the shared alert | 2. User B opens the same alert | 3. Both attempt to acknowledge near-simultaneously | 4. Inspect final state and audit
Test_Data: Shared alert; Users A and B
Expected_Result: The alert is acknowledged exactly once with a consistent final state; the audit log records the actual acknowledging user without duplicate or conflicting entries
